title: Senator McCortney Again Denies SB13 a Fair Hearing
author: James Silberman
orientation: An urgent appeal published days before Oklahoma's February 27, 2020 committee deadline, reporting that Senate Health and Human Services chair Greg McCortney left SB13 (a bill to abolish abortion) off the final committee agenda despite massive public pressure. The piece warns McCortney of long-term political and historical consequences and asks readers to call and email him to demand a hearing.
audiences: Oklahoma constituents in McCortney's Ada-area district
Pastors and church leaders weighing civic engagement
Political historians and journalists covering state-level abolition efforts

questions.question: What concrete legislative event was the article reacting to?
questions.answer: The agenda for the final scheduled Senate Health and Human Services committee meeting before the February 27 deadline was published without SB13 listed, meaning the bill would die in committee unless McCortney altered the agenda or called a special hearing.
questions.quote: The agenda for Monday’s Senate Health and Human Services committee meeting has been [published](http://www.oksenate.gov/committees/meetingnotices.aspx) and Senate Bill 13 is not on it. Monday is the final scheduled committee meeting prior to the February 27 deadline for bills to be passed out of committee.

questions.question: What forms of public pressure had already been brought to bear on McCortney?
questions.audience: Oklahoma constituents in McCortney's Ada-area district
questions.answer: A capitol rally of thousands, a statewide petition of nearly 2,000 Oklahomans, hundreds of petitions specifically from his own district, the Oklahoma Republican Party platform, and resolutions from the Oklahoma Baptists and Free Will Baptists had all called on him to grant SB13 a hearing.
questions.quote: On February 11, thousands showed up at the capitol to demand SB13 receive a hearing. He was delivered a petition of nearly 2,000 Oklahomans demanding SB13 receive a hearing. This morning, he was delivered hundreds of petitions from the people of his district (Ada area) demanding SB13 receive a hearing. The platform of the Oklahoma Republican Party demands SB13 be given a hearing. The Oklahoma Baptists and Free Will Baptists have both passed official resolutions demanding SB13’s passage and enforcement.

questions.question: On what theological and constitutional grounds does the article claim SB13 is uniquely necessary?
questions.audience: Pastors and church leaders weighing civic engagement
questions.answer: The author argues that the U.S. and Oklahoma constitutions require equal protection of the law for all people, which only SB13 would extend to the preborn, and that God Himself demands the abolition of child sacrifice.
questions.quote: The Constitution of the United States and the State of Oklahoma demand equal protection of the law be provided to all people, something which no bill other than SB13 accomplishes. The Creator of the Universe demands iniquity like child sacrifice be abolished.

questions.question: How does the author frame McCortney's likely place in the historical record?
questions.audience: Political historians and journalists covering state-level abolition efforts
questions.answer: Silberman predicts abortion will eventually be abolished and that history will single out the pro-life Republican leaders and committee chairs who delayed that abolition, casting McCortney as one of the figures responsible for prolonging the killing.
questions.quote: The history books will tell that story, and part of that story is the pro-life Republican leaders and committee chairs who delayed abolition and perpetuated the killing for an extra few years. Tragically, this is the role McCortney is currently writing for himself in the story – a story his grandchildren and great grandchildren will read.

questions.question: Was there still a procedural off-ramp for McCortney at the time of writing?
questions.answer: Yes. The article notes McCortney could amend the agenda up to 24 hours before the meeting or call a special hearing on Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday of the following week to consider SB13 before the deadline.
questions.quote: He can make changes to the agenda up until 24 hours prior to the meeting. He can call a special hearing on Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday of next week to hear SB13 prior to the deadline.

questions.question: What specific action does the article ask readers to take?
questions.audience: Oklahoma constituents in McCortney's Ada-area district
questions.answer: Readers are urged to call and email McCortney immediately at the published addresses, pleading with him to take the warnings seriously and grant SB13 a hearing before time runs out.
questions.quote: But time is running out. He needs to act quickly. So please call and email him now, pleading with him to take seriously the admonitions he’s receiving and give SB13 a hearing.

questions.question: How does the author characterize McCortney's apparent motives or self-awareness?
questions.answer: Silberman suggests McCortney does not grasp the gravity of his choice and warns that the decision will dog him personally and politically for the rest of his life, not just in the next election cycle.
questions.quote: McCortney likely doesn’t grasp the seriousness of what he’s choosing. If he did, he would choose differently. This is not something that he will be able to weather. There will be political repercussions, but more significantly this will follow him the rest of his life.

related.title: Why "No Bill But SB13" ?
related.reason: Lays out the strategic and theological case for why SB13 is the only acceptable Oklahoma legislation, providing the framework that makes McCortney's denial of a hearing significant.

related.title: Anti-SB13 Campaigners Aren't Ignorant. They're Trying to Deceive You.
related.reason: Documents the broader pro-life establishment opposition to SB13 that gave political cover to gatekeepers like McCortney.

related.title: Motion to Bring SB13 to Senate Floor Coming Tomorrow! Email Your Senator!
related.reason: Covers the next-step procedural maneuver after McCortney's committee blockade, attempting to bypass him by bringing SB13 directly to the Senate floor.

related.title: National Right to Life Endorses Greg McCortney, the Senator Who Kept Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Direct follow-up reporting on the political fallout and pro-life establishment rewards McCortney received for the very behavior described here.

related.title: Greg McCortney: Abortion Rights Man of the Year
related.reason: Year-end retrospective on McCortney's role in killing SB13, building directly on the events foreshadowed in this article.

related.title: The Inexhaustible Cowardice of Oklahoma Senate President Greg Treat
related.reason: Parallel critique of another Oklahoma Republican Senate leader obstructing abolition legislation, showing the systemic pattern McCortney participated in.

related.title: Pastor and Gubernatorial Candidate Dan Fisher Responds to Oklahoma Baptist Leaders Opposing SB13
related.reason: Provides background on the Oklahoma Baptist resolutions referenced in this article as part of the moral pressure campaign on McCortney.
